body.label: source_id.root.theorem
body.claim: For every natural number n, either n is even or n+1 is even.

body.text: Proof. We prove this by induction on n. For n=0, n is even. For the induction step, assume that either n is even or n+1 is even. We split into cases. If n is even, then n=2k for some natural number k, hence n+2=2(k+1) is even, so the right side of the desired disjunction holds. If n+1 is even, then the left side of the desired disjunction holds.
